2026-03-28T04:18:34.3095201Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3095764Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3096197Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3096578Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3096903Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3097141Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3097316Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3097502Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3097685Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3097934Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3098145Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3098445Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-03-28T04:18:34.3098711Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-03-28T04:22:02.1707116Z ● Save PR performance findings to cache memory (shell)
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1709185Z   │ cat > /tmp/gh-aw/cache-memory/dash-pr-history.json << 'EOF'
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1710147Z   │ {
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1710498Z   │ "entries": [
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1710855Z   │ {
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1711641Z   │ "pr": 37882,…
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1712011Z   └ 18 lines...
2026-03-28T04:22:02.1712593Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4142267Z Review posted. Here's a summary of findings:
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4142900Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4145213Z **2 Medium, 1 Low** performance issues found in the new code:
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4145657Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4147864Z 1. **Medium — `partitionKeyToIdMapper.ts` line 10**: The `` `${partitionCount}:${partitionKey}` `` cache key allocates a new heap string on **every** call (including cache hits), partially defeating the purpose of the cache. A two-level `Map<string, Map<number, number>>` avoids this.
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4149640Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4152119Z 2. **Medium — `partitionKeyToIdMapper.ts` line 15**: The LRU delete+reinsert pattern runs two Map write ops on every cache hit. Since partition keys are typically stable for the life of a producer, a write-once or lazy-LRU strategy removes this per-send overhead.
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4153844Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4155511Z 3. **Low — `dataTransformer.ts` line 155**: `body.trim()` allocates a new string for every received message body in `looksLikeJson`. For `{`, `[`, and `"` (the dominant cases), an index scan for the first non-whitespace character avoids any allocation.
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4157031Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4275783Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:08.4276261Z Total usage est:        1 Premium request

2026-03-28T04:22:12.2474300Z Agent: Now I have a clear picture of all the changes. Let me analyze them for performance issues:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2474748Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2474936Z Agent: 1. **`dataTransformer.ts`**:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2475622Z Agent:    - Added fast paths for string, number, boolean encoding (avoids `JSON.stringify` for simple cases) ✅ Good perf improvement
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2476749Z Agent:    - Added `looksLikeJson` heuristic to skip `JSON.parse` for non-JSON-looking strings ✅ Good perf improvement
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2477662Z Agent:    - `looksLikeJson` calls `body.trim()` on every received message body - potential issue if messages are very large strings, but generally fine
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2479027Z Agent:    - The `stringNeedsJsonEscaping` regex test trades off regex overhead against `JSON.stringify`, which is generally acceptable, though the `trim()` call creates unnecessary string allocations for messages with whitespace padding.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2479987Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2480173Z Agent: 2. **`eventDataBatch.ts`**:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2480772Z Agent:    - Added `_rawMessages: RheaMessage[]` to avoid decode+re-encode in idempotent mode ✅ Good perf improvement
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2481890Z Agent:    - The `_rawMessages` array stores references that parallel the main messages array, which should keep memory overhead minimal.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2482483Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2482670Z Agent: 3. **`eventHubSender.ts`**:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2483177Z Agent:    - Skip object spread/clone when no tracing properties needed ✅ Good perf improvement
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2483648Z Agent:    - Clean implementation, no issues.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2483923Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2484122Z Agent: 4. **`partitionKeyToIdMapper.ts`**:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2484952Z Agent:    - Added module-level cache with a composite key that accounts for partition count variations across different clients, so the cache should handle multiple connection scenarios correctly.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2486395Z Agent:    - The LRU eviction strategy using delete and re-insert on every cache hit adds overhead since even read operations trigger Map mutations, though this is likely acceptable for partition key lookups at typical scale.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2487625Z Agent:    - The cache grows unboundedly across all instances until hitting the 100-entry limit, which is fine but worth keeping in mind.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2488235Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2488422Z Agent: 5. **`partitionReceiver.ts`**:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2488721Z Agent:    - Added event
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2488922Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2489541Z Agent: -driven wake-up through `QueueSignal` instead of polling. The signal mechanism uses a `Set` of waiter callbacks that get triggered when events arrive.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2490597Z Agent:    - The queue check happens synchronously before waiting, so if i... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 7390 chars]

2026-03-28T04:22:12.2493671Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2494225Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, repo: azure-sdk-for-js, path: sdk/eventhub/event-hubs/src/partitionRec..., ref: refs/pull/37882/head)
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2494767Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2495071Z Agent: Now I have a clear picture of all the changes. Let me now analyze the performance issues:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2495667Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2495963Z Agent: **1. `partitionKeyToIdMapper.ts`** (line 10 and 14-15):
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2497944Z Agent: - **Medium**: Every cache lookup (hit or miss) constructs a template literal string `${partitionCount}:${partitionKey}`, creating a new heap string on each call. Since the purpose of this cache is to avoid `Buffer.from` allocations in `hashPartitionKey`, but the cache key construction itself allocates a new string for every call (including hits), this partially undermines the optimization for very high-frequency calls with the same key.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2499807Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2499989Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2501533Z Agent: - **Medium**: Cache hits trigger a delete-and-reinsert pattern to maintain LRU ordering, which means two Map operations on every single lookup of a frequently accessed key. This could be optimized with a threshold-based approach or a simpler marking mechanism to reduce the constant overhead.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2502811Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2503075Z Agent: **2. `dataTransformer.ts`** (line 155 - `body.trim()`):
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2504353Z Agent: - **Low**: `looksLikeJson(bodyStr)` calls `body.trim()` on every message, allocating a new string unnecessarily. For high-throughput scenarios with large payloads, a character-by-character scan to find the first non-whitespace character would avoid this allocation entirely.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2505512Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2505763Z Agent: **3. `eventDataBatch.ts`** (lines 149-152, 427-429):
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2507233Z Agent: - **Low**: Storing both the encoded `Buffer` and the raw `RheaMessage` for each event in idempotent batches doubles the in-flight memory footprint. Since `RheaMessage` objects carry headers, properties, and body references, this becomes significant at scale, though it's an intentional tradeoff for idempotency guarantees.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2508679Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2509745Z Agent: Looking at the specific line numbers and weighing the impact, the LRU cache's delete-and-reinsert pattern on every hit stands out as the most problematic, followed by the template literal allocation in the cache key generation and the string trimming in the JSON detection logic.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2510834Z 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2511330Z Agent: Now I have a full picture of all changes. Let me post the performance review findings.

2026-03-28T04:22:12.2521386Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2521911Z Agent: **2 Medium, 1 Low** performance issues found in the new code:
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2522261Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2523483Z Agent: 1. **Medium — `partitionKeyToIdMapper.ts` line 10**: The `` `${partitionCount}:${partitionKey}` `` cache key allocates a new heap string on **every** call (including cache hits), partially defeating the purpose of the cache. A two-level `Map<string, Map<number, number>>` avoids this.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2524595Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2525717Z Agent: 2. **Medium — `partitionKeyToIdMapper.ts` line 15**: The LRU delete+reinsert pattern runs two Map write ops on every cache hit. Since partition keys are typically stable for the life of a producer, a write-once or lazy-LRU strategy removes this per-send overhead.
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2526933Z Agent: 
2026-03-28T04:22:12.2528804Z Agent: 3. **Low — `dataTransformer.ts` line 155**: `body.trim()` allocates a new string for every received message body in `looksLikeJson`. For `{`, `[`, and `"` (the dominant cases), an index scan for the first non-whitespace character avoids any allocation.

Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["path","line","body"],"properties":{"path":{"type":"string","description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). For multi-line comments, this is the ending line."},"body":{"type":"string","description":"Review comment content in Markdown. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. {"timestamp":"2026-03-28T04:19:17.166700722Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":37882,\"title\":\"[event-hubs] Reduce JavaScript Event Hubs send and receive hot-path overhead\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nThis PR improves several internal JavaScript Event Hubs send and receive hot paths.\\n\\nIncluded changes:\\n\\n- fix double encoding in idempotent batches\\n- cache repeated partition key hashing results\\n- wake receive waits from queue notifications before polling fallback\\n- add fast paths for string and primitive payload encoding\\n- skip JSON parsing for bodies that do not look like JSON\\n- avoid cloning `EventData` when no tracing properties are present\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n- Reduce duplicate encoding work in idempotent producer paths.\\n- Cache repeated partition-key hash inputs instead of reallocating conversion buffers for the same keys.\\n- Make receive-side waiting event-driven before falling back to polling.\\n- Add cheaper send-side payload transformation paths for strings and simple primitives.\\n- Avoid unconditional JSON parsing for received bodies that do not look like JSON.\\n- Skip tracing-related object cloning when no instrumentation properties need to be added.\\n\\n## Validation\\n\\n- Full package Node test suite passed with `npm run test:node` in `sdk/eventhub/event-hubs`.\\n- The checked-in benchmark harnesses were used as directional validation for both send-side and receive-side changes.\\n- `js-benchmark/benchmark.mjs` now reports receive subscription initialization, warm-up, send-call time, and matched-event callback latency separately, uses bounded client retry timeouts, and inserts short cool-down periods between send-heavy sections to avoid emulator throttling windows.\\n- Recent local emulator runs showed stable matched-event callback latency around `30-31 ms` for both the receive-latency and receive-decode cases once those throttle windows were separated from the measured path.\\n\\n## Risk\\n\\nRisk is low to moderate and localized to internal send/receive hot paths.\\n\\n- Most changes are allocation and fast-path optimizations without public API changes.\\n- The receive queue wake-up change is the most behavior-sensitive item, but it is covered by the package test suite.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"draft\":false,\"merged\":false,\"mergeable_state\":\"blocked\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/37882\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"videlalvaro\",\"id\":30834,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/videlalvaro\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30834?v=4\"},\"labels\":[\"customer-reported\",\"Event receive subscription initialization, warm-up, send-call time, and matched-event callback latency separately, uses bounded client retry timeouts, and inserts short cool-down periods between send-heavy sections to avoid emulator throttling windows.\\n- Recent local emulator runs showed stable matched-event callback latency around `30-31 ms` for both the receive-latency and receive-decode cases once
